description: The fabricated-number memo: the thesis asserts a twenty-session return of 14 percent, but the cited evidence records a different figure — '14' appears nowhere in the cited corpus, while '0' and '4' are genuinely grounded. The grounding metric (the production verifier, reused verbatim) must fail this bundle; everything else about the memo is fine.

memo.thesis: AVGO stays on the watchlist: the twenty-session return of 14 percent argues real demand, but momentum-v1 failed its gates with 0 of 4 folds passing, so the desk waits for validated coverage before committing capital.
kill_criteria: The twenty-session return turns negative and the close falls below the SMA50
The xsmom-pit family verdict is revoked at a quarterly review
memo.dissent: Waiting is its own decision: names with this relative strength tend to rerate before gates catch up, and the watchlist has historically been where the desk parks calls it lacks the conviction to make.

evidence: AVGO daily closes (EODHD vendor bars, split-adjusted): latest close 172.35 on 2026-07-10, previous close 170.10, 20 sessions ago 158.32. Window: 60 sessions ending 2026-07-10.
DCP indicators for AVGO as of 2026-07-10: SMA20 168.40, SMA50 161.75, 20-day return 8.86 percent, last close 172.35.

Quant validation record as of 2026-07-10: family momentum-v1 FAILED decision gates on real data (null-model p 0.830, deflated Sharpe negative, 0 of 4 walk-forward folds passed); family xsmom-pit holds a decision-grade PASS on the S&P 500 point-in-time universe (4 of 4 folds, null p 0.000, deflated Sharpe 0.998). AVGO is not in the current xsmom-pit winner decile; no validated strategy currently covers AVGO.
